mission.title: Install and Configure llm-d
mission.description: llm-d is a high-performance distributed inference serving stack optimized for Kubernetes, enabling state-of-the-art performance for large language models across various hardware accelerators. It is ideal for production environments requiring efficient orchestration of model servers like vLLM and SGLang, with intelligent scheduling and autoscaling capabilities. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for adequate resources to handle high-scale traffic.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the llm-d command-line interface, which is necessary for managing deployments.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d

```

> This command downloads the CLI binary, makes it executable, and moves it to your PATH.
commands: curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new GKE cluster with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create llm-d-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Replace 'us-central1' with your preferred region.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your local kubeconfig to connect to the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command fetches the credentials for the cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks that the nodes in the cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes in a 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Install llm-d Helm Chart
steps.description:
This step installs the llm-d application using Helm, deploying it in the 'llm-d' namespace.

```bash

helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/ && helm repo update

kubectl create namespace llm-d

helm install llm-d llm-d/llm-d --namespace llm-d --version 0.5.1

```

> Adds the llm-d Helm repository.
> Creates a namespace for llm-d.
> Installs llm-d with the specified version.
commands: helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/ && helm repo update

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for the llm-d deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/autoscaling.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> Applies the autoscaling configuration.
> Applies the monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall llm-d
uninstall.description:
This step removes the llm-d deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

helm uninstall llm-d --namespace llm-d

kubectl delete namespace llm-d

```

> Uninstalls the llm-d release.
> Deletes the llm-d namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade llm-d
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data. This step involves draining nodes, upgrading the Helm release, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade llm-d llm-d/llm-d --namespace llm-d --version 0.5.1

kubectl get pods -n llm-d

```

> Replace <node-name> with the name of the node you want to drain.
> Upgrades llm-d to the specified version.
> Verifies that the pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image may not exist or is inaccessible.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure it is available in the specified registry.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application may be misconfigured or missing required environment variables.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' and verify the configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough resources allocated for the deployment.

**Fix:**
Increase the node size or number of nodes in the cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service may not be running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Verify the service status with 'kubectl get services -n llm-d' and check the deployment.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new hardware accelerators and improved autoscaling features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods -n llm-d' showing all pods in 'Running' state.
codeSnippets: curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d
helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/ && helm repo update

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the required tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
